note: Mirrors model/data/external/benchmarks.json global_comparables. Same magnitude class, five-orders-of-magnitude loss spread: exposure and vulnerability dominate, not magnitude.

events.name: Kobe (Great Hanshin), Japan
events.loss_note: ~US$130B total: >US$100B property/infrastructure + up to US$50B disruption (Toyoda 2008); ~2.3% of Japan GDP in capital-stock damage
events.context: dense modern urban area directly above rupture

events.name: Haiti (Port-au-Prince)
events.loss_note: US$7.8B PDNA (govt/World Bank DALA) — >120% of Haiti's 2009 GDP; academic estimates US$8.1-13.9B (Cavallo et al. 2010)
events.context: extreme vulnerability: weak building regulation, dense informal housing

events.name: Bohol, Philippines
events.loss_note: PHP 2.257B monetized infrastructure (NDRRMC SitRep 35) — LOWER BOUND; 73,002 houses damaged counted in units
events.context: semi-rural island province; our backtest event

wvf_anchor.name: West Valley Fault scenario (this tool)
wvf_anchor.note: World Bank ~US$48B total-loss estimate; PHIVOLCS 2013: PHP 2.4T building damage. Scenario, not an occurred event.
